nodes.x: Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife.

Now for its opposite : — Homes miserable, faces cloudy, tyrant husbands, “ nagging” wives, squalling, filthy, disobedient ohildren, houses dirty and slatternly, grumblings, contentions, fault-findings, angry words, blows ! Hell in embryo ! Who would not shun it ? Who would not flee from it ?

comparative inactivity, and attempt only to build log houses or mud shanties. If you had the spirit of your calling, you would be anxious to build the best houses you could, and make the best gardens, fields, and vineyards, though you knew that you would not enjoy them one day after they were completed. I will here quote the words of the Savior—“And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends of…
In mine ears said the Lord of hosts, Of a truth many houses shall be desolate, even great and fair, without inhabitant.
Lest when thou hast eaten and art full, and hast built goodly houses, and dwelt therein;
A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.
Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.
thee from iniquity, and that thou shouldst become to Him a son, and a servant, and a minister of His presence.
And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.
The fining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold: but the Lord trieth the hearts.
As the fining pot for silver, and the furnace for gold; so is a man to his praise.
As silver is melted in the midst of the furnace, so shall ye be melted in the midst thereof; and ye shall know that I the Lord have poured out my fury upon you.
Behold, I will stir up the Medes against them, which shall not regard silver; and as for gold, they shall not delight in it.
Behold, I have refined thee, but not with silver; I have chosen thee in the furnace of affliction.
Your gold and silver is cankered; and the rust of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.
A wicked doer giveth heed to false lips; and a liar giveth ear to a naughty tongue.
My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.
I said, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me.
Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth, though he hide it under his tongue;
¶ A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth.
Deliver my soul, O Lord, from lying lips, and from a deceitful tongue.
Whoso mocketh the poor reproacheth his Maker: and he that is glad at calamities shall not be unpunished.
He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker: but he that honoureth him hath mercy on the poor.
And calamity shall cover the mocker, and the scorner shall be consumed; and they that have watched for iniquity shall be hewn down and cast into the fire.
He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he.

A great man, possessing a noble sool is above meanness; he never stoope from the dignity of bis reputation to the degradation of a traitor, to accomplish the purposes of God. — Nor does he endeavor to win the hearts of the people, under the mantle of charity, with the oil of religion, while his tongue *s green with the venom of corruption, and his pockets groan with the weapons of death. O thou…
Children’s children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers.
Both young men, and maidens; old men, and children:
Lo, children are an heritage of the Lord: and the fruit of the womb is his reward.
The glory of young men is their strength: and the beauty of old men is the gray head.

by no less a personage than the one I have named. That Prophet who had such great power while he remained on the earth—that had power to call down fire upon his enemies—that had power to call fire from heaven and consume the sacrifices—that Prophet who was wafted to heaven in a chariot of fire—that same august personage has been sent from the eternal worlds with this important message to the…
Excellent speech becometh not a fool: much less do lying lips a prince.
The words of a wise man’s mouth are gracious; but the lips of a fool will swallow up himself.
The lip of truth shall be established for ever: but a lying tongue is but for a moment.
A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are the snare of his soul.
Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise: and he that shutteth his lips is esteemed a man of understanding.
A fool’s lips enter into contention, and his mouth calleth for strokes.
A gift is as a precious stone in the eyes of him that hath it: whithersoever it turneth, it prospereth.
Behold thou hast a gift, and blessed art thou because of thy gift. Remember it is sacred and cometh from above—
A man’s gift maketh room for him, and bringeth him before great men.
He that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends.
He that loveth not knoweth not God; for God is love.

The man who willeth to do well , we should extol his virtues and speak not of his faults behind his back . A man w ho willfully turneth away from his friend without a cause is not easily forgiven. The kindness of a man should never be forgotten . That person who never forsaketh his trus t should ever have the hi ghest place for regard in our hearts , and our love shou ld never fail but incr ease…
He that is void of wisdom despiseth his neighbour: but a man of understanding holdeth his peace.
He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.
A reproof entereth more into a wise man than an hundred stripes into a fool.
Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
A wise man feareth, and departeth from evil: but the fool rageth, and is confident.
A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in till afterwards.
The wise in heart will receive commandments: but a prating fool shall fall.
Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him.
An evil man seeketh only rebellion: therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.
Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
If he say thus, It is well; thy servant shall have peace: but if he be very wroth, then be sure that evil is determined by him.
Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:
Let a bear robbed of her whelps meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.
I will meet them as a bear that is bereaved of her whelps, and will rend the caul of their heart, and there will I devour them like a lion: the wild beast shall tear them.
Would to God ye could bear with me a little in my folly: and indeed bear with me.

"As we approached the timber, a large black bear came out towards us. We were not afraid of him, for we were on the Lord's business, and had not mocked God's prophets as did the forty-two wicked children who said to Elisha, *Go up thou bald head,' for which they were torn by bears. When tne bear got within eight rods of us he sat on his haunches, looked at us a moment, and ran away ; and we went…

She said nothing; she only thought what if her beloved had been torn to
pieces by the bears in his attempt to pass to the castle. But she would
not say this to him, lest she waken his fears for her, a weak woman; she
must always pass to the church through such perils.
Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
¶ Woe to him that coveteth an evil covetousness to his house, that he may set his nest on high, that he may be delivered from the power of evil!
He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: cut them off in thy truth.
Yet he also is wise, and will bring evil, and will not call back his words: but will arise against the house of the evildoers, and against the help of them that work iniquity.
If he has desired to do evil, and has not repented in his days, behold, evil shall be done unto him, according to the restoration of God.
Evil pursueth sinners: but to the righteous good shall be repayed.
The beginning of strife is as when one letteth out water: therefore leave off contention, before it be meddled with.
Cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out; yea, strife and reproach shall cease.
A wrathful man stirreth up strife: but he that is slow to anger appeaseth strife.
The lot causeth contentions to cease, and parteth between the mighty.
He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the just, even they both are abomination to the Lord.
The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.
And he leadeth away the righteous into precious lands, and the wicked he destroyeth, and curseth the land unto them for their sakes.
The Lord trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.
The Lord preserveth all them that love him: but all the wicked will he destroy.

Yea, all his iniquities and abominations, and all the wars, and contentions, and bloodshed, and the stealing, and the plundering, and the committing of whoredoms, and all manner of iniquities which cannot be enumerated—telling them that these things ought not to be, that they were expressly repugnant to the commandments of God.
Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing he hath no heart to it?
Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
Wisdom resteth in the heart of him that hath understanding: but that which is in the midst of fools is made known.
Wisdom is too high for a fool: he openeth not his mouth in the gate.
For what hath the wise more than the fool? what hath the poor, that knoweth to walk before the living?
For wisdom is a defence, and money is a defence: but the excellency of knowledge is, that wisdom giveth life to them that have it.
A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
A man that hath friends must shew himself friendly: and there is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.
Thine own friend, and thy father’s friend, forsake not; neither go into thy brother’s house in the day of thy calamity: for better is a neighbour that is near than a brother far off.
A man void of understanding striketh hands, and becometh surety in the presence of his friend.
A slothful man hideth his hand in his bosom, and will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.
He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.
Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?
He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
He loveth transgression that loveth strife: and he that exalteth his gate seeketh destruction.

And he shall go forth, suffering pains and afflictions and temptations of every kind; and this that the word might be fulfilled which saith he will take upon him the pains and the sicknesses of his people.
He maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth; he breaketh the bow, and cutteth the spear in sunder; he burneth the chariot in the fire.

He that walketh righteously, and speaketh uprightly; he that despiseth the gain of oppressions, that shaketh his hands from holding of bribes, that stoppeth his ears from hearing of blood, and shutteth his eyes from seeing evil;
He that passeth by, and meddleth with strife belonging not to him, is like one that taketh a dog by the ears.
He that hath a froward heart findeth no good: and he that hath a perverse tongue falleth into mischief.
He that walketh in his uprightness feareth the Lord: but he that is perverse in his ways despiseth him.
Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.
He that hath clean hands, and a pure heart; who hath not lifted up his soul unto vanity, nor sworn deceitfully.
He that diligently seeketh good procureth favour: but he that seeketh mischief, it shall come unto him.
Frowardness is in his heart, he deviseth mischief continually; he soweth discord.
He that begetteth a fool doeth it to his sorrow: and the father of a fool hath no joy.
He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow: but a prating fool shall fall.
He that troubleth his own house shall inherit the wind: and the fool shall be servant to the wise of heart.
A fool hath no delight in understanding, but that his heart may discover itself.
The heart of him that hath understanding seeketh knowledge: but the mouth of fools feedeth on foolishness.
The foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the Lord.
A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.
A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance: but by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken.
Make me to hear joy and gladness; that the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.
All the days of the afflicted are evil: but he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast.

Spiritual Healing By Elder James E. Faust Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles I humbly come to this pulpit today to speak about a sure cure for heartache, disappointment, torment, anguish, and despair. The psalmist stated, “He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds” ( Ps. 147:3 ). The healing is a divine miracle; the wounds are a common lot of all mankind. Shakespeare has said,…
A wicked man taketh a gift out of the bosom to pervert the ways of judgment.
And for this cause I said that he is a wicked man, for he has sought to take away the things wherewith you have been entrusted; and he has also sought to destroy your gift.
Wherefore, a man being evil cannot do that which is good; neither will he give a good gift.
For behold, if a man being evil giveth a gift, he doeth it grudgingly; wherefore it is counted unto him the same as if he had retained the gift; wherefore he is counted evil before God.
The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more, when he bringeth it with a wicked mind?
Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, He taketh the wise in their own craftiness.
Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
A foolish son is a grief to his father, and bitterness to her that bare him.
A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.
A foolish son is the calamity of his father: and the contentions of a wife are a continual dropping.
He that wasteth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a son that causeth shame, and bringeth reproach.
The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.

The  son,  perchance,  has  reached  his  maturity;  he  is  the  master  of 
his  own  destiny;  a  free  agent  unto  himself.  The  father  is  power- 
less to  control  by  force  or  to  direct  by  arbitrary  command;  and, 
while  he  would  gladly  make  any  effort  or  sacrifice  to  save  his  son 
from  the  fate  nnpending,  he  fears  for  what  seems  to  be  an  awful 
certainty. …
Also to punish the just is not good, nor to strike princes for equity.
And they shall scoff at the kings, and the princes shall be a scorn unto them: they shall deride every strong hold; for they shall heap dust, and take it.
To bind his princes at his pleasure; and teach his senators wisdom.
By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.
That he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.
That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.
And to another, that he may teach the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.
For behold, to one is given by the Spirit of God, that he may teach the word of wisdom;
Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise: and he that shutteth his lips is esteemed a man of understanding.
The words of wise men are heard in quiet more than the cry of him that ruleth among fools.
Yea also, when he that is a fool walketh by the way, his wisdom faileth him, and he saith to every one that he is a fool.
